messages.content:
1. A sequence of business and information links is called a ________?

Logistics network

Distribution network

Sales channel

Supply chain
Supply chain

2. Supply chains link operators to suppliers in __________?

Layers

Groups

Tiers

Channels
Tiers

3. Activities which are undertaken before the final operation are termed _______ activities.

Upstream

Primary

Secondary

Downstream
Upstream

4. In the automotive supply chain information flows in which direction?

Towards the end user only

Towards the supplier only

Between the end user and the operation only

In both directions between the end user and the supplier
In both directions between the end user and the supplier

5. How do automotive companies mainly sell their new products?

Over the internet

Through retail distributors

Via a trade magazine

Directly to customers
Through retail distributors

6. Selecting suppliers and purchasing items is called ________?

Negotiation

Procurement

Contracting

Selection
Procurement

7. The main benefit to a materials processing operation of having multiple sourcing of component parts is ______?

Consistent quality

Speed of delivery

Continuity of supply

Flexibility of products
Continuity of supply

8. Which of the following is an advantage of single sourcing to an operation?

Continuity of supply

Ability to keep prices down

Increased flexibility of supply

Lower administration cost
Lower administration cost

9. When an organization owns its suppliers it is known as ________?

Downstream supplier collaboration

Upstream vertical integration

Downstream vertical integration

Upstream supplier collaboration
Upstream vertical integration

10.Logistics is the term for which activities?

Procurement of raw materials

Distribution of goods to customers

Accounting for raw materials in transit

All of the above
Distribution of goods to customers
